c.d: The number of active users or organizations using the app, indicating its popularity and adoption.
The number and variety of third-party apps and services the app can integrate with to streamline workflows.
Availability of essential collaboration functions such as chat, video conferencing, file sharing, task management, and real-time document editing.
The strength of security measures, including data privacy options and advanced security features suitable for sensitive organizational use.
Cost structure including free plans, subscription tiers, and value for money.
Ease of use, user interface design, and learning curve affecting how efficiently teams can adopt the app.
